questions.question: The radius drawn to the point of contact of a tangent to a circle is always?
questions.explanation: Radius is perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn to a circle from an external point?
questions.explanation: Exactly two tangents can be drawn from an external point.

questions.question: If a tangent is drawn to a circle, which of the following is true?

options.A: It cuts the circle at two points
options.B: It touches the circle at exactly one point
options.D: It lies completely inside the circle

questions.explanation: A tangent touches the circle at only one point.

questions.question: Which of the following is the longest chord of a circle?
questions.explanation: Diameter is the longest chord.

questions.question: If two tangents are drawn from an external point to a circle, they are?
questions.explanation: Tangents from an external point are equal.

questions.question: The common point between a tangent and a circle is called?
questions.explanation: The tangent touches the circle at the point of contact.

questions.question: Which of the following always passes through the centre of a circle?
questions.explanation: Diameter passes through the centre.

questions.question: A line which intersects a circle at two distinct points is called?
questions.explanation: A secant cuts the circle at two points.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is always true?
questions.explanation: Every diameter is a chord.

questions.question: The distance from the centre of a circle to a tangent is equal to?
questions.explanation: Distance from centre to tangent equals radius.

questions.question: Which part of a circle is curved?
questions.explanation: An arc is curved.

questions.question: How many centres does a circle have?
questions.explanation: A circle has exactly one centre.

questions.question: Which of the following lies completely outside the circle?
questions.explanation: A tangent lies outside except at the point of contact.

questions.question: If the radius of a circle is r, the length of the diameter is?
questions.explanation: Diameter = 2 × radius.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a chord of a circle?

options.C: Line joining two points on the circle

questions.explanation: Radius has only one endpoint on the circle.

questions.question: The region enclosed between a chord and the corresponding arc is called?
questions.explanation: That region is called a segment.

questions.question: The region enclosed by two radii and the arc between them is known as?
questions.explanation: This region is called a sector.

questions.question: Which statement about tangents is correct?

options.B: Tangents intersect the circle at two points
options.C: Tangents are perpendicular to radius at point of contact

questions.explanation: Key property of tangents.

questions.question: Which of the following figures has no straight edges?
questions.explanation: Circle has only a curved boundary.

questions.question: Which of the following always lies inside the circle?
questions.explanation: Centre always lies inside.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for all circles?

options.D: All diameters are equal to radius

questions.explanation: All radii of a circle are equal.

questions.question: How many points of contact does a tangent have with a circle?
questions.explanation: Tangent touches at exactly one point.

questions.question: Which of the following does NOT pass through the interior of the circle?
questions.explanation: Tangent touches but does not pass through interior.

questions.question: A secant becomes a tangent when?
questions.explanation: When it touches at exactly one point.

questions.question: Which statement is always correct?

options.C: All diameters of a circle are equal

questions.explanation: All diameters of the same circle are equal.
